scope.summary: Implement a self-contained Tailor project archive export/import (.hsk-garment ZIP bundle: GarmentSpec JSON + material presets + UV atlas + trim meshes + animation cache refs + garment_version chain) as the portable session-restore and team-sharing artifact
acceptance_criteria: POST /tailor/garments/:id/archive exports a .hsk-garment ZIP containing: spec_json (GarmentSpec), material_preset.json (FabricProperties snapshot), uv_atlas.png (if present), trim_meshes/ (OBJ per trim), animation_manifest.json (frame range + fps + cache paths), and MANIFEST.json listing all contained files with SHA-256 hashes

POST /tailor/garments/import_archive with a .hsk-garment file reconstructs the garment in a new tailor_garments row with status=draft, re-attaches material presets and trims, and emits TailorGarmentDraftProposed

A round-trip (export then import on a clean DB) produces a GarmentSpec that is semantically equivalent to the original (same panels, seams, fabric, trim placements) and passes PANEL_CLOSED + SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH validation
The archive format version is stamped in MANIFEST.json; GarmentSpec forward migration (MT-139) is invoked on import if the archive version is older than the current schema
scope.risk_if_missed: Without a portable project archive, garment sessions are locked to a single Handshake PostgreSQL instance. Teams cannot share garment work across instances, operators cannot back up and restore individual garments, and the tool cannot be used in disconnected or multi-site workflows.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
